author: thepointsguy
text: The Amex Platinum is absolutely worth the $695 annual fee for most travelers in 2026. The lounge access alone pays for it.
dataPoint: On CreditOdds approval data, a 720 score with 4+ hard inquiries in the last 12 months is denied for the Amex Platinum more than half the time.

author: nerdwallet
text: 7 no-annual-fee credit cards you should consider adding to your wallet this year.

author: onemileatatime
text: Which is the better beginner travel card: the Chase Sapphire Preferred or the Capital One Venture X?
dataPoint: CreditOdds ran the marginal value on both for a light traveler: the Venture X only wins once you use more than about $200 of its $300 travel credit.

author: milestomemories
text: What's the single easiest premium credit card to get approved for right now?
dataPoint: CreditOdds approval data shows the easiest-premium ranking flips dramatically based on number of recent inquiries, not just credit score.

author: nerdwallet
text: Capital One has agreed to a $190 million settlement over its 360 savings account rates. Here's how to check if you're owed money.

author: thepointsguy
text: The President's new proposal to cap credit card interest rates at 10% could reshape the entire rewards industry. Democrats and Republicans are split.

author: UpgradedPoints
text: Hot take: annual fees on travel cards are getting out of control and most people don't come close to breaking even.
